source_pr.title: privileges: use UPPER case for privileges in SHOW GRANT
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #26275

Problem Summary:
MySQL displays the privileges like `SELECT`, `PROCESS` etc. in Upper case, while TiDB in Capital case `Select`, `Process` etc.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:
The output from SHOW GRANTS and information_schema.USER_PRIVILEGES now show the privileges in UPPER case.

How it Works:
Just fold any privilege (from parser/mysql.Priv2Str) to upper case.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [X] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [X] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- SHOW GRANTS and information_schema.USER_PRIVILEGES now outputs the privileges in UPPER case.

symptoms: tk.MustQuery(`SELECT * FROM information_schema.user_privileges WHERE grantee="'usageuser'@'%'" ORDER BY privilege_type`).Check(testkit.Rows("'usageuser'@'%' def BACKUP_ADMIN NO", "'usageuser'@'%' def SELECT YES")) (executor/infoschema_reader_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery(`SELECT * FROM information_schema.user_privileges WHERE grantee="'usageuser'@'%'" ORDER BY privilege_type`).Check(testkit.Rows("'usageuser'@'%' def BACKUP_ADMIN NO", "'usageuser'@'%' def SELECT YES")) (executor/infoschema_reader_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR is primarily a correctness/compatibility change (privilege formatting in SHOW GRANTS / information_schema.USER_PRIVILEGES). The available evidence does not indicate a targeted flaky-test stabilization change with a clear intermittent failure signature, so we conservatively classify it as insufficient evidence.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).
